Rapid Response Network · Santa Clara County · Since 2016

Protecting our community through deportation defense

Free, confidential support — 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. If you see ICE, if someone is detained, or if you have an upcoming check-in — we are here.

📞 Call Now: 408-290-1144
Learn how we help →

24/7 Emergency Hotline
408-290-1144
Available in all languages
Free · Confidential
How can we help you today?
Path A · Call Now
I see ICE activity in my neighborhood
We activate trained responders to verify, document, and monitor. Call the hotline immediately.
Path B · Call Now
My loved one has been detained by ICE
We connect your family to free emergency legal services and support within hours.
Path C · Call Now
I have an upcoming ICE check-in or court date
We provide trained volunteers to accompany you so you don't have to go alone.

Page Notes & Design Decisions
Our priority

Hotline number in three places

408-290-1144 appears in the nav, hero, and gold banner so someone in crisis sees it immediately without scrolling.

Colors

Gold = urgent action, Navy = trust

Gold is used only for hotline CTAs and key buttons. Navy anchors the credibility of the page. Light gold (#FFFBB6) is background-only — too pale for text.

Content

Three cards framed as situations, not services

"I see ICE," "my loved one was detained," "I have a check-in" — written for someone unfamiliar with RRN who needs to recognize their situation fast.

Bilingual

Language toggle always visible in nav

EN | ES stays in the navbar on every page. Would love the team's input on whether we should prioritize any other languages beyond Spanish.

📄 Page 2 of 5

About Page

Builds trust and explains the structure of RRN — who we are, who our partners are, and how the system works. Secondary audience: media, funders, partner orgs.

Coordinated community defense
since 2016

SCC RRN is a countywide collaborative of 10 nonprofit organizations coordinating free, emergency response services for community members impacted by federal immigration enforcement in Santa Clara County.

What is SCC RRN?
10
Partner orgs
7
Service areas
2016
Founded

Governed by a Steering Committee with representatives from each partner organization. Partner organizations collectively deliver all RRN services. Five full time-RRN staff and seven service areas coordinated across the county.

How It Works
1
Community calls the 24/7 hotline
408-290-1144
2
Dispatcher triages the call
Routes to responders, attorneys, or accompaniment volunteers
3
Community is protected
Trained volunteers respond, document, and support
Serving All of Santa Clara County

Our hotline and services are available to all 15 cities and unincorporated communities in Santa Clara County — no matter where you are.

Page Notes
Content

Leading with credibility

I opened with "since 2016" and the 3-stat row so visitors immediately understand our scale and history without reading a paragraph.

Design

Gold mission statement block

The gold quote section is meant to be a moment that sticks. I want visitors to leave the About page with one clear sentence about who we are.

Content

Partner pills → could be logos

These are text pills for now. If we want to swap with a logo grid we can do that.

📄 Page 3 of 5

Get Help Page

The most critical page for community members in crisis. Should load fast, be readable on a phone, and put the hotline number front and center before anything else.

🌐 Bilingual priority: This page should be the FIRST translated. Most urgent visitors are Spanish-speaking.

EN | ES
📞 Call Hotline

We're here to help — call us now

Our hotline is free, confidential, and available 24 hours a day. We speak English and Spanish and have interpretation available in all languages.

24/7 Emergency Hotline
408-290-1144
Available in all languages
Free · Confidential · Call anytime
When should I call?
🚨 ICE Activity Nearby
You see immigration agents on the street, at a home or business, or received a report of activity. Call immediately — we deploy trained community observers.
⚖️ Someone Was Detained
A loved one has been arrested or detained by ICE. We connect your family to our free emergency legal services within hours — same day if the arrest just happened when you called our hotline.
📅 Upcoming Check-In or Court
You or a loved one has an upcoming ICE check-in, ISAP appointment, or immigration court date. We send trained volunteers to accompany you. You won't go alone.
We serve all of Santa Clara County
— including:
San José
Santa Clara
Sunnyvale
Mountain View

Los Altos

Morgan Hill
Gilroy
Monte Sereno
Los Altos Hills
Stanford (unincorp.)
San Martin (unincorp.)
+ Other unincorporated areas

Not in Santa Clara County? Find your local rapid response network at www.ccijustice.org/carrn

Know Your Rights — Share this with your family
🚪
Do not open the door
You are not required to let agents in without a warrant signed by a judge.
🤐
You have the right to remain silent
Do not answer questions about your immigration status or country of origin.
✍️
Do not sign anything
You have the right to speak with an attorney before signing any documents.
🚶
If you're outside, ask "Am I free to go?"
If yes, leave calmly. You don't have to answer questions outside your home either.
Other Resources
Immigration Legal Services
Free and low-cost legal help for non-emergency immigration needs. → SCC Legal Services Directory
Bay Area RRN Network
Outside Santa Clara County? Find your local rapid response network. → California RRNs Directory
211 Santa Clara
For non-immigration social services needs — housing, food, health, and more. → Dial 2-1-1

[email protected]
Page Notes
Priority

Red = urgency, hotline front and center

This is the most important page for people in crisis. I used red for the hero so visitors immediately know they're in the right place. The hotline number is the first thing they see.

Mobile

Hotline number must be tappable

On mobile the number needs to open the dialer directly. In Squarespace, set the button URL to tel:4082901144 — don't use plain text for the number anywhere on this page.

Content

KYR section — worth sharing

Community members often want to forward this to family. The downloadable bilingual KYR card linked here would make that easy. We already have the KYR card design through ILRC.

📄 Page 4 of 5

Get Involved Page

For people who want to give their time or money. Focused entirely on action — volunteering, hosting, and donating. No professional inquiries here (those live on Contact & Media).

📞 Call Hotline

Join us. Our community
is stronger together.

Whether you want to volunteer on the front lines, bring a training to your community, or support the network financially — there is a place for you here.

Ways to Volunteer
🏃 Become a Rapid Responder
Attend a free Rapid Responder training and join our network of over 3,000 community volunteers who monitor and document ICE activity in real time.

What you'll do: Respond to ICE alerts in your area, observe and document activity, and keep our community informed.

Open to all Santa Clara County community members. No experience required. Training is free and takes about 2 hours.
Sign up for a training →
📚 Host a Workshop at Your Organization
Bring RRN to your community. We offer two types of free workshops:

Know Your Rights Training — for community members and families. We come to you.

RRN Informational Workshop — for organizations, churches, schools, and businesses that work with impacted communities.

Both are free, bilingual English & Spanish, and available countywide.
Request a workshop → outreach@sccrrn.org

Support our work

Your donation keeps the hotline running 24/7, funds free legal services for detained community members, and supports families in crisis. Every dollar goes directly to community defense.

SCC RRN is fiscally sponsored by Asian Law Alliance, a 501(c)(3). All donations are tax-deductible.

Donate →
© 2026 RRN Santa Clara County
[email protected] — workshops & volunteering
Page Notes
Content

Two ways to volunteer, one way to donate

I separated volunteering and donating into distinct sections — they're different commitments and shouldn't compete visually. The donate section is full-width at the bottom so it stands on its own.

Content

outreach@sccrrn.org is the only contact here

All workshop requests and volunteer questions go to outreach@.

Content

Events section — staff can update independently

Using Squarespace's native Events block means anyone on the team can add or remove trainings without touching the rest of the site. Each event should include date, time, location, language, and a signup link.

Note

No social links on this page

Social follows live on the Home page Stay Informed section — that's the right context for them. This page stays focused on action: volunteer, host a workshop, donate.

Contact & Media Page

For journalists, partner organizations, and professional inquiries only. Clear purpose: get the right person to the right email, and give media everything they need without a phone call.

Contact & Media
EN | ES
📞 Call Hotline

Contact the right person

RRN has two public contact emails — one for media and one for outreach. Use the guide below to make sure your message reaches the right team.

⚠️ For immigration emergencies: call the hotline at 408-290-1144. Do not email for urgent situations — email is not monitored 24/7.
📰 Media & Press
For journalists, reporters, and media organizations requesting interviews, statements, background information, quotes, or data about RRN's work or ICE activity in Santa Clara County.

Response time: Within 24 hours on business days.
Please include: Your name, outlet, deadline, and story description.
communications@sccrrn.org →
🤝 Organizational Partnerships
For organizations — nonprofits, government agencies, employers, schools, faith communities — interested in formally partnering with RRN, co-sponsoring events, or integrating RRN services into your work.

Note: To request a workshop or volunteer training for your org, use the Get Involved page instead.
leadership@sccrrn.org →
Press Kit — Key Facts for Media
One-line description
SCC RRN is a countywide coalition of 10 organizations providing 24/7 emergency response to federal immigration enforcement in Santa Clara County since 2016.
Services
24/7 hotline · Free emergency legal services · ICE activity monitoring & documentation · Accompaniment to check-ins & court · Family support
Key numbers
10 partner orgs · 5 full-time staff · Operating since 2016 · Serving all of Santa Clara County
Partner organizations
Amigos de Guadalupe · Pangea Legal · SIREN · CARAS · Catholic Charities · LUNA · Human Agenda · Pars Equality Center · Asian Law Alliance · Step Forward
Hotline

communications@sccrrn.org — press & partnerships
Page Notes
Purpose

This page is just for press and partnerships

I kept this focused — no donation links, no social buttons. Anyone landing here is a journalist or potential partner, and they need to find the right contact fast.

Content

Three emails, each with a clear lane

communications@sccrrn.org is for press. leadership@sccrrn.org is for organizational partnerships. outreach@sccrrn.org is for volunteers and workshops and lives on Get Involved only. I want each page to have one clear contact so nothing gets lost.

Action item

Fill in [NAME] and [ORG] before publishing

We need to confirm who our designated media contact is and make sure they're comfortable being listed publicly. Happy to discuss who makes the most sense for this role.

🎨 Design Reference

Design System & Build Notes

A reference for whoever builds our site in Squarespace — covering color usage, typography, email routing, and component patterns. Please don't change these without checking in first.

🎨 Our Color System

Gold
#F2C422
Navy
#0B2346
Light Gold
#FFFBB6
Navy Mid
#1B3A5C
Alert Red
#C0392B
Off-white
#F8F6F0
Gold (#F2C422)
Our primary brand color. Use for CTA buttons, the hotline banner, section accents, and stat numbers. Never use for body text — it fails contrast on white backgrounds.
Navy (#0B2346)
Our trust color. Use for the navbar, footer, hero backgrounds, headings, and body text. Should feel dominant and authoritative.
Light Gold (#FFFBB6)
Background tints only — unincorporated area pills, highlight boxes. Too light for text or buttons. Never use as a CTA color.
Alert Red (#C0392B)
Used only on our Get Help page hero. It signals urgency without being alarming. Loses impact if used anywhere else — keep it exclusive to that page.
Off-white (#F8F6F0)
Alternating section backgrounds and card backgrounds. Warmer than pure white — intentional, so our site feels community-centered rather than clinical.

✍️ Our Typography

Display / Headings — DM Serif Display
Protecting our community
through deportation defense
Body / UI — DM Sans
Our hotline is free, confidential, and available 24 hours a day. We speak English and Spanish and have interpretation available in all languages.
Labels / Eyebrows — DM Sans Bold Uppercase
24/7 Emergency Hotline · in Santa Clara County
Hotline Number — DM Serif Display Large
408-290-1144

Both fonts are free via Google Fonts. Load them in Squarespace under Design → Fonts. DM Serif Display for all headings (H1–H3), DM Sans for all body text, labels, and UI elements.

📧 Our Email Routing — Three Lanes, No Overlap

communications@sccrrn.org
For press, media inquiries, and organizational partnerships. Lives on our Contact & Media page only. Never route media to the hotline or to outreach@.
outreach@sccrrn.org
For volunteer questions and workshop requests. Lives on our Get Involved page only. Not listed anywhere else on the site.
leadership@sccrrn.org
For organizational partnerships. Listed on the Contact & Media page alongside communications@. Keep these two clearly labeled so partners know which to use.
408-290-1144 (hotline)
For anyone needing direct help — ICE activity, detention, check-ins. Appears in the announcement bar on every page, the Get Help hero, and the Home page hero. Always displayed as a tappable link (tel:4082901144) on mobile.

🗂️ Our Five Pages

Home (sccrrn.org)
Our front door. Hotline number in three places, three situation-based cards, stats row, Stay Informed section with social follow buttons, partner org pills.
About (/about)
Our story and structure. Stats (10 partner orgs, 7 service areas, since 2016), how we work, service areas, partner list, city coverage for all of Santa Clara County including Stanford and San Martin.
Get Help (/get-help)
Most critical page. Red hero for urgency. Hotline prominently displayed. Three situation cards, Know Your Rights section, FAQ, downloads, city coverage, and link to SCC legal services directory.
Get Involved (/get-involved)
Two volunteer paths (Rapid Responder, Host a Workshop via outreach@sccrrn.org), events block for trainings, full-width donate section. Fiscally sponsored by Asian Law Alliance, a 501(c)(3).
Contact & Media (/contact)
For press and partners only. Two contact cards (communications@ for media, leadership@ for partnerships), press kit facts, single media contact placeholder [NAME] [ORG].

📐 Squarespace Build Notes

Announcement bar
Our gold hotline bar must appear on every page. Set it in Website → Pages → Website Tools → Announcement Bar. Text: "📞 408-290-1144 · All languages · Free · Confidential"
Our logo
Upload as PNG with transparent background via Design → Logo & Title. The current logo has a black background — we need a transparent version for it to sit cleanly on the navy navbar.
Phone number links
Every instance of 408-290-1144 must use href="tel:4082901144" so it opens the dialer on mobile. Never use plain text for our hotline number.
Bilingual pages
Use Squarespace's translated pages feature or duplicate with /es/ URL prefix. Our Get Help page (/es/obtener-ayuda) should be translated first — that's the page our community needs most in Spanish.
Events block
Use Squarespace's native Events page type on Get Involved. Our team can add and remove trainings without touching any other part of the site. Each event: title, date/time, location, language, registration link.
Donate button
Link to our external donation page, open in a new tab. In Squarespace: Button block → URL → check "Open in new window." Subtitle should read: "SCC RRN is fiscally sponsored by Asian Law Alliance, a 501(c)(3). All donations are tax-deductible."
Instagram embed
Add via Edit Page → Add Block → Instagram. Connect our account (scc_rapidresponsenetwork). Shows our latest posts automatically — no manual updates needed. Lives on the Home page Stay Informed section only.
Social links
Instagram: instagram.com/scc_rapidresponsenetwork · Facebook: facebook.com/RRNSCC. Social follow buttons appear on the Home page Stay Informed section only — not on Get Involved or Contact & Media.
Downloadable PDFs
Upload our hotline card and KYR card via Squarespace Files. Link the download buttons to the file URLs. These appear as downloads on mobile and open in browser on desktop.
Legal services link
The "Immigration Legal Services" resource on Get Help links to: https://desj.santaclaracounty.gov/oir/legal-services — the SCC legal services directory. Check this URL periodically to make sure it's still live.
City coverage
Our city pills appear on About and Get Help pages. Order: San José, Santa Clara, Sunnyvale, Mountain View, Palo Alto, Milpitas, Campbell, Los Gatos, Los Altos, Saratoga, Cupertino, Morgan Hill, Gilroy, Monte Sereno, Los Altos Hills, Stanford (unincorp.), San Martin (unincorp.), + Other unincorporated areas.

📱 Mobile Priorities

Get Help page
Our hotline number and a "Call Now" button must be visible without scrolling on mobile. Test on a real phone before we publish — this is the page someone will open in a crisis.
Font sizes
Squarespace auto-scales fonts for mobile. Check that our hotline number (large DM Serif Display) doesn't overflow or wrap awkwardly on smaller screens.
Partner pills & city pills
The flex-wrap layout handles mobile automatically. Confirm pills don't get too small to read on a 375px wide screen — minimum readable pill size is about 80px wide.
Last updated 3/11/26 by Mariana Haro
